description_long: A cluster of four large, daisy-like flowers with pale yellow petals and prominent brownish-orange centers is shown, each flower radiating outward in a symmetrical, radial pattern. The petals are elongated, slightly pointed, and arranged in a single whorl, with some overlapping at the base. The flowers are supported by slender green stems, which are visible beneath the blooms and extend downward. Several green leaves with a lanceolate to ovate shape are interspersed among the stems, adding a natural, leafy texture to the arrangement. The overall appearance is vibrant and fresh, reminiscent of wildflowers commonly found in urban parks or landscaped gardens. The coloration is dominated by soft yellows, greens, and hints of brown, giving the object a natural and inviting look.
description: A cluster of four pale yellow, daisy-like flowers with brown centers and green stems, accented by lanceolate green leaves. The flowers are arranged in a natural, radial pattern, evoking a wildflower bouquet.
description_view_0: Front view showing all four flowers facing forward, with petals radiating outward and green leaves visible at the base.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth of the flower cluster, with overlapping petals and stems visible in profile.
description_view_2: Rear three-quarter view showing the backs of some flowers and more of the green leaves and stems.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with a focus on the side petals and the layered arrangement of the flowers.
attribute_petal_arrangement: The petals are arranged in a radial, single-whorled configuration, with each flower displaying symmetrical, evenly spaced petals that slightly overlap at the base. The arrangement is typical of composite or daisy-like flowers, with clear radial symmetry and no visible layering beyond the single whorl.
